sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Introduce an info action that sketches ideas for reforming Cardano treasury governance and invites public discussion and collaboration.
Stated amount: not stated in document

Deliverables: Basic propositions for treasury governance reform, A proposed treasury governance voting sequence, A proposed thematic budget structure, Discussion of strategic entity and expert commission roles

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal claims current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and gridlock among governance coalitions.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal argues treasury governance must provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano growth and stability.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states treasury governance should be explicitly addressed and supported by the governance system and the Cardano constitution.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposal argues the NCL should not be treated as the overall budget because it is only a vague initial approximation and cannot replace a coherent treasury governance process.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal recommends a dedicated investment budget that includes strategic direction, a roadmap, thematic structure, and allocation percentages across domains.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims current treasury withdrawal voting has created perceived competition for scarce resources, hostility, toxicity, and gridlock among governance coalitions.
Independent evidence for: The proposal recommends appointing a strategic entity by vote for a fixed term to prepare, propose, and implement budgets.
Independent evidence for: The proposal recommends appointing an expert commission by vote for a fixed term to evaluate and select projects that meet budgetary requirements.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal argues treasury governance must provide clear direction, a coherent strategy, and a specific roadmap for Cardano growth and stability." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says Cardano treasury governance is in a poor state due to a funding impasse and insufficient direct support from the existing governance system." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
